pr.title: slight fix to function in stage.c
pr.author: snuffysasa

summary: Corrected the signature and implementation of Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset in the stage system: it no longer takes an unused f32 argument or returns that argument, and is now declared/defined as void while still writing camera offset values into the provided Vec.
what_changed: Changed Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset in src/melee/gr/stage.c from returning f32 and accepting Vec* plus f32 arg8 to returning void and accepting only Vec*.
Removed the unused arg8 parameter and the artificial return arg8 statement from Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset.
Kept the actual side effects of the function: assigning stage_info.cam_info.cam_x_offset to vec3->x, stage_info.cam_info.cam_y_offset to vec3->y, and 0.0F to vec3->z.
Updated the declaration in src/melee/gr/stage.h to match the corrected void Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset(Vec* vec3) signature.
smart_moves: Recognized that the previous return value and second parameter were likely decompilation scaffolding rather than real function behavior.
Kept the header and source signature synchronized, preventing mismatched prototypes after the decomp-matching fix.
Reduced the function to its observed purpose: mutating an output Vec with stage camera offset data.
decomp_lessons: If a function only writes through an output pointer and the supposed return value is just an untouched parameter, revisit the inferred signature; it may actually be void with fewer parameters.
Matching nearby or caller functions can expose fake parameters or fake returns introduced during earlier decompilation attempts.
Small signature fixes in source must be mirrored in headers to keep later decompilation and call-site cleanup consistent.
A function named with SetVec-style behavior is plausibly side-effect-only; do not preserve dummy return plumbing unless call-site evidence requires it.
naming_conventions: The existing tentative name Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset was retained.
Stage function naming in this area uses the Stage_ prefix and camera-related terms such as Cam, Offset, Bounds, and PauseCamZPos.
The Vec pointer parameter remained named vec3; the removed f32 parameter had been a generic placeholder name arg8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a dummy f32 parameter and return that did not contribute to the function's actual side effects, a common matching tactic when a previous prototype was overfit to compiler artifacts.
Converted the function to void because its meaningful behavior is storing three float fields into the output Vec.

The PR body says the author found this function while matching other functions, suggesting cross-function matching evidence drove the correction, but no detailed assembly or caller evidence was included in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find call sites of Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset to confirm they all expect only side effects on Vec and no return value.
Look for other Stage_* setter-like functions that still return unused arguments or preserve artificial f32 parameters.
Review neighboring camera helper functions in src/melee/gr/stage.c for additional prototype cleanup opportunities.
